claim: Loop engineering is the orchestration layer that triggers agents at the right time (cron, webhooks, other agents) and manages state/memory across multi-session work.
actionable: Design loop infrastructure that decouples agent triggering from prompt engineering; use triggers (cron, events) and shared state storage instead of hardcoding workflows in prompts.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Compounding loops multiply agent value: when multiple autonomous loops (support, SEO, growth) read and write to shared artifact folders (signals, tasks, docs), they amplify each other's insights.
actionable: Build multiple specialized agent loops that all read/write to a shared file system; design signals (feedback, ideas, bugs) that flow across loops.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Context engineering for agents requires setting up a legible, executable, work-tree-friendly, verifiable codebase where agents can understand what to change and test their work.
actionable: Document codebase structure in CLAUDE.md; provide dev scripts (no-cost startup); ensure agents can run tests and capture videos of changes before submitting PRs.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Use artifact abstractions (docs, signals, tasks, tickets) with README.md schemas for each type; agents create and link artifacts so downstream loops can consume them.
actionable: Define artifact types (e.g., signals for observations, tasks for work items) with schema READMEs; have all agents read/write these standard types.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Create loop contracts (README files per loop) documenting goal, workflow, timeline, and outstanding tasks so agents understand context when triggered and can log progress.
actionable: For each autonomous agent loop, write a contract that explains the loop's goal, execution steps, task backlog, and historical actions; agents read this before acting.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Don't ask agents to self-verify their work; instead spawn a read-only verifier agent to validate changes and log results in PR skew files.
actionable: When designing agent PR workflows, require a separate verification agent rather than having the same agent judge its own work.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Prompt engineering + prompt caching + context management are insufficient; harness engineering includes agent loop design, state tracking, tool orchestration, and environment setup.
actionable: Beyond prompt optimization, invest in harness infrastructure: state machines, artifact systems, loop orchestration, and environment legibility.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Three loop ingredient: triggers (cron, webhooks, other agents), file/state structure (artifact types, schemas), and tool/connector access. Setup + verification is most important.
actionable: When building autonomous loops, prioritize: (1) clear triggers, (2) well-documented artifact types, (3) necessary tools. Setup codebase for agent self-verification first.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: For support loops, pull tickets every 30 minutes, let agent respond and log frictions/ideas as signals; downstream growth loops read signals and implement fixes autonomously.
actionable: Run a support loop hourly; log all feedback as signals in a shared folder that growth/product loops monitor for opportunities and bugs.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real

claim: Use Playwright for agent testing and video capture of UI changes; attach videos to PRs so humans can easily verify agent work without reading code diffs.
actionable: Set up Playwright in your codebase; have agents record UI test videos and attach them to PRs for human verification.
source_title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
